info.name: Fleet Command API
info.description:
All API routes for the Fleet Command Express server (default port 5001).

Collection variables:
• {{base_url}} — http://localhost:5001  (change if running remotely)
• {{token}}    — populated automatically by the Login request's test script

Workflow:
1. Run **Auth › Login** — the test script saves the token to {{token}}.
2. All other requests use {{token}} automatically via the Authorization header.

item.name: Payments
item.description: Payment queue and cron digest trigger. Queue shows all vendors with approved-but-unpaid recon work and their isDue status based on current digest settings. Manual trigger uses forceAll=true — fires all due vendors regardless of current hour.

request.description: Returns all vendors with approved-but-unpaid recon work. Each batch includes isDue (whether the vendor should receive a digest now based on cron settings), recipients[], paymentTerms, jobs[], and totals. Vendors with no pending work are excluded.

Manually fires the vendor digest email to ALL vendors with pending work, bypassing the isDue schedule check (forceAll=true). Emails are logged with triggered_by=manual so they appear without the ⏰ auto badge in Email Log. Admin / tech_support only.

item.name: Settings
item.description:
Site-wide settings stored in the site_settings table (key/value). Changes take effect immediately — the cron scheduler reads these at each tick, no server restart needed.

Cron-related keys:
• digest_daily_hours  — comma-separated hours (24h) when completion-terms vendors receive digests (default: "8,12,17")
• digest_weekly_day   — day name when weekly-terms vendors receive digest (default: "Friday")
• digest_weekly_hour  — hour (24h) on that day (default: "17")

Read-only virtual key (not stored in DB):
• server_timezone     — sourced from TZ env var (default: America/Phoenix)

request.description: Returns all site_settings rows as a key→value map, plus the virtual server_timezone key derived from the TZ env var. Any role can call this.

Sets the hours (comma-separated, 24h) when the cron fires digests for completion-terms vendors. Examples: "8,12,17" = 8am/12pm/5pm, "9,14" = 9am/2pm. Takes effect on the next cron tick (top of the hour) — no restart needed.

Sets the day of the week when weekly-terms vendors receive their digest. Must be a full English day name (Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday). Takes effect on the next cron tick — no restart needed.
Sets the hour (24h integer, stored as string) on the weekly digest day when weekly-terms vendors fire. E.g. "17" = 5pm, "9" = 9am. Takes effect on the next cron tick — no restart needed.
Upserts any arbitrary key in site_settings. Replace :key in the URL with the setting name. Uses ON CONFLICT upsert so safe to call repeatedly.
